truth_evidence.fact: Idempotence requirement: re-running a completed stage with no partial deltas is a no-op.
--no-resume flag does not affect Stage 1 (no resume files).

verdict_detail: Stage 1 stateless / JSON-only output; idempotent rerun matches truth facts on Stage 1 having no resume state.
notes: JSON-only outputs are durable artifacts, not resume scratch.

truth_evidence.fact: Local-FS atomic write idiom: write payload to *.tmp, fsync, then os.rename/os.replace.
File ordering invariant: write payload (.pt) BEFORE manifest (.json).

verdict_detail: Claim says 'atomic checkpointing' for both .json and .pt but at line 284 does not state the .tmp+rename mechanism nor the .pt-before-.json ordering invariant. Both invariants ARE stated in §11 (RES-0015, RES-0018) so the spec is internally complete, but this specific line does not self-contain the atomicity definition.
missing_invariant: Local atomicity mechanism (.tmp+os.replace) and payload-before-manifest ordering
notes: Resolved at §11 lines 695, 710 — flag as incomplete only on this line.

truth_evidence.fact: On resume, completed layers are loaded directly from _stage{N}_partial/; failed layers re-run from prior stage's output.

verdict_detail: Replay-from-partial-files plus pre-merge model guard (num_routed_experts) is a sound idempotence/state-validation pattern consistent with the per-layer completion idiom.
notes: Guard is an additional safety mechanism beyond the canonical idiom.

truth_evidence.fact: Existence of .json implies the .pt payload is fully on disk (payload-before-manifest).

verdict_detail: Remap-before-snapshot ordering matches the broader payload-before-manifest discipline; persisting pre-merge keys would corrupt downstream Stage 3 inputs as claimed.
notes: Domain-specific invariant; truth-faceted by the general ordering discipline.

truth_evidence.fact: HF Jobs bucket FUSE writes are NOT durable on cancel/timeout.
Spec §11 mandates per-stage Hub commit; partial checkpoints under _stage{N}_partial/ are local-FS scratch only.

verdict_detail: Per-layer B/C covariance spill is to local FS (_stage3_bcov_partial/, _stage3_ccov_partial/) inside the pod, treated as scratch. Durability boundary for the stage is the per-stage Hub commit (RES-0014). Background I/O claim is a perf detail, not a durability claim.
notes: Local-FS spill paths are not bucket FUSE writes; durability handed off to Hub commit on stage exit.

truth_evidence.fact: Idempotence: completed layers loaded directly from _stage{N}_partial/.
Existence of payload file => payload fully on disk.

verdict_detail: Skip-on-spill-existence is the canonical resume idiom for Stage 3 Phase A; relies on atomic write meaning a present file is a complete file.

truth_evidence.fact: Per-stage Hub commit is the durability boundary; bucket / local scratch is not.

verdict_detail: Cleaning the spill dir on success is safe because by then the durable Hub commit (Stage 3 repo) has been made; spill files were scratch.

truth_evidence.fact: Per-stage Hub repo upload is the durability boundary.
Local-FS atomic write idiom (.tmp + rename).

verdict_detail: Originals snapshot is durable when committed to the Stage 3 Hub repo per RES-0014. Local atomicity for the .pt covered by RES-0015. RES-0020 strengthens with ordering (snapshot before α/factoring).

truth_evidence.fact: Local-FS atomic write idiom (.tmp + rename).
format_version mandatory on every partial checkpoint.
On resume, completed layers loaded directly; failed layers re-run from prior stage.

verdict_detail: Per-layer atomic .pt with format_version=1 and re-run-from-Stage3 fallback matches the canonical idiom and the format_version policy.
notes: Single-file checkpoint (no separate manifest) is acceptable: the .pt is both payload and manifest, format_version inside it serves the manifest role.

truth_evidence.fact: Per-stage Hub repo upload is the durability boundary.
HF Hub commits are durable when HTTP commit returns 200 OK.

verdict_detail: Sidecar deletion (originals + input covariance) is safe because both are already committed to per-stage Hub repos (Stage 2 for cov sidecar, Stage 3 for originals sidecar).
notes: Relies on prior-stage Hub commit having succeeded; spec assumes this.

truth_evidence.fact: Local-FS atomic write idiom (.tmp + rename).
format_version mandatory on every partial.

verdict_detail: Step-boundary checkpoints every 100 steps with rolling window=2 plus partial-batch grad-drop is a sound idempotence-without-accumulation strategy. Atomicity covered by §11.
notes: Rolling window of 2 trades disk for safety against a single corrupt file.

truth_evidence.fact: Stage 6 idempotence: stateless; teacher eval cache persists across runs as JSON in artifacts (also uploaded to Hub).

truth_evidence.fact: Bucket FUSE writes are NOT durable on cancel/timeout.
Bucket is scratch space; Hub repo is the only durable boundary.
Per-stage Hub commit before next stage; resume from Hub, not bucket.

verdict_detail: Spec statement is the canonical durability rule from auto-memory.

truth_evidence.fact: Each pipeline stage producing a durable artifact must call upload_large_folder to a Hub repo before the job exits.

verdict_detail: Per-stage Hub repos for stages 2–5 + bucket-as-scratch matches the auto-memory durability policy.

truth_evidence.fact: Local-FS atomic write idiom: write payload to *.tmp, fsync the file (and parent directory), then os.rename / os.replace.
POSIX rename(2) atomicity guarantee.
fsync of the file (and parent directory) is required BEFORE rename for crash-durability.
Dangling .tmp cleanup at stage startup.

verdict_detail: Spec correctly states .tmp + os.replace and dangling .tmp cleanup, but does NOT mention the fsync-before-rename requirement. Without fsync of file and parent directory, rename can be reordered with the data write and a power-loss/kernel-panic crash can leave the renamed file pointing at zero-length or stale data (truth_ref L18). For the SIGKILL-only threat model the spec defends against, os.replace alone is sufficient; for power-loss/kernel-panic the fsync invariant is needed.
notes: Threat model is restricted to SIGKILL/timeout per §11; if power-loss is in scope this is a durability gap.

truth_evidence.fact: --no-resume flag disables all within-stage resume; Stage 1 and Stage 6 unaffected.

verdict_detail: Verbatim match with truth fact.

truth_evidence.fact: Per-stage idempotence: completed layers loaded from partial; failed layers re-run.
--no-resume disables partial I/O.

verdict_detail: Stage 3 resume granularity description (covariance phase + α search; --no-resume deletes spills + skips α cache) matches the resume idiom and --no-resume semantics.

truth_evidence.fact: File ordering invariant: write payload (.pt) BEFORE manifest (.json).

verdict_detail: Stage 2 layer-completion = both .json AND .pt; orphan .pt without .json => delete and reprocess. Matches payload-before-manifest discipline. The orphan-delete mechanism handles the crash-between-snapshot-and-json case correctly.
notes: Strong claim; this is the model-correct interpretation of the ordering invariant.

truth_evidence.fact: Idempotence: re-run of a completed phase is a no-op.

truth_evidence.fact: Idempotence: rerun of completed stage is a no-op (i.e. detected and refused if not safe).
